---
title: "Introduction"
---

## A typical paragraph

Prose is plain Markdown. The shared CSS gives it a serif body face, a
narrow measure, and a small accent colour for links. Inline math is
written as $f(x) = ax^2 + bx + c$, and displayed equations as

$$\int_{-\infty}^{+\infty} e^{-x^2}\,dx = \sqrt{\pi}.$$

Multi-line aligned math uses the `align` environment:

$$\begin{aligned}
E[X+Y] &= E[X] + E[Y], \\
\operatorname{Var}(X+Y) &= \operatorname{Var}(X) + \operatorname{Var}(Y) + 2\,\operatorname{Cov}(X, Y).
\end{aligned}$$

## A figure rendered from code

```{r}
#| label: fig-gaussian
#| fig-cap: "Two Gaussian densities with different scales."
#| fig-width: 5
#| fig-height: 3

curve(dnorm(x, 0, 1), from = -4, to = 4,
      ylab = "density", xlab = "x",
      lwd = 2, col = "#b8123e")
curve(dnorm(x, 0, 1.5), add = TRUE, lwd = 2, lty = 2, col = "#2c3e50")
legend("topright", bty = "n",
       legend = c(expression(sigma == 1), expression(sigma == 1.5)),
       col = c("#b8123e", "#2c3e50"), lwd = 2, lty = c(1, 2))
```

The chunk above is rendered as PNG in the HTML build (set globally in
`Notes/_metadata.yml`) and as PDF in the print build.

## A small table

| Distribution | Support | Mean | Variance |
|:-------------|:-------:|:----:|:--------:|
| Bernoulli$(p)$ | $\{0, 1\}$ | $p$ | $p(1-p)$ |
| Binomial$(n, p)$ | $\{0, \ldots, n\}$ | $np$ | $np(1-p)$ |
| Poisson$(\lambda)$ | $\mathbb{Z}_{\geq 0}$ | $\lambda$ | $\lambda$ |
| Normal$(\mu, \sigma^2)$ | $\mathbb{R}$ | $\mu$ | $\sigma^2$ |

## Sources

End every chapter with a *Sources* section listing the lecture and the
textbook chapters it draws on.
